site.title: Local Civic Reference
site.pageTitle: Gage County Candidate Directory | Local Civic Reference
site.description: A neutral candidate reference based on cited public sources for selected 2026 races appearing in Gage County.

hero.headline: Not every local race lists party affiliation on the ballot.
hero.body: This guide brings together candidates who appear on ballots across Gage County, along with publicly documented affiliation information and the sources used to verify it. It is intended as a factual reference for voters preparing for Election Day.

directory.heading: Candidate Directory
directory.noScriptBody: This reference uses JavaScript to load the published civic data. Enable JavaScript to view the directory.

guide.heading: How to read this directory
guide.introduction: Purpose, sourcing, review dates, and corrections are collected here for readers who want more context.
guide.noticeBody: This guide presents factual candidate information using a consistent editorial standard. Appearance in the directory is not an endorsement, recommendation, ranking, or evaluation of any candidate.
guide.verificationIntroduction: Different sources establish different facts. Candidate cards identify the source used for each displayed claim.
guide.correctionIntroduction: Candidate cards link each displayed fact to its source and show the source review date.
guide.correctionPrompt: If an entry appears inaccurate or outdated, send a supporting public source to:

context.heading: What “nonpartisan” means here
paragraphs: A nonpartisan race is one in which party labels do not appear beside candidate names on the ballot. A candidate may still have a publicly documented political affiliation.
Affiliation is one piece of information, not a complete description of a candidate. It does not predict every decision, and local questions may not divide along conventional party lines.
The county and state drawers include supplemental races that may use different ballot rules. Their inclusion does not mean every race in this directory is officially nonpartisan.

items.question: Why are these races called nonpartisan?
items.answer: Party labels do not appear beside candidate names on the ballot. Rules vary by office and jurisdiction.

items.question: Does a listed affiliation mean the candidate is officially running as a party nominee?
items.answer: Not necessarily. The directory includes races with different ballot rules. A publicly documented political affiliation should not be treated as proof of nomination status; consult official election materials for the specific race.

items.question: How was this information verified?
items.answer: See How information is verified for the source types and the facts each establishes.

items.question: Why are some candidates listed under primary history?
items.answer: They participated in the primary but did not advance. They remain visible as election history, separate from current listings.

items.question: What happens if an affiliation changes?
items.answer: The entry should be updated when a reliable public source documents the change. Each card shows when its source was reviewed.

items.question: Does this site endorse candidates?
items.answer: No. Appearance in the directory is not an endorsement, recommendation, ranking, or evaluation.

items.question: Why might a voter consider this information relevant?
items.answer: Some voters consider political affiliation alongside a candidate’s experience, qualifications, statements, and priorities when evaluating people who may make decisions about public budgets, services, or tax levies.

items.question: How can an error be corrected?
items.answer: Send a supporting public source to the correction contact shown in this guide.

footer.heading: Local Civic Reference
footer.purpose: An independent candidate reference. This resource is not an official election authority.
footer.authorityNotice: Consult the appropriate official election authority for ballot details, polling information, deadlines, and final candidate lists.

repositoryLink.label: Project source and documentation available on GitHub.

interfaceCopy.emptyDirectory: No candidate entries are currently available.
